editor_settings.title: Which Is Better, Fujifilm X100V or X100VI?
editor_settings.content:
Which Is Better, Fujifilm X100V or X100VI?

The short answer is Fujifilm X100VI.

The smarter answer is a little more annoying: X100VI is the better camera, but X100V can still be the better buy if the used price is low enough.

Quick Verdict

If you are choosing between them from scratch, the X100VI is the stronger camera overall. It keeps the same fixed 23mm f/2 lens and core X100 feel, but adds a 40.2MP sensor, in-body image stabilization, 20 film simulations, and stronger video specs.

If you already own an X100V, the answer changes. The X100VI is not so much better that every X100V owner should panic and upgrade.

Why the X100VI Wins Overall

The upgrades are real, not cosmetic.

  • X100VI uses a 40.2MP APS-C sensor, while X100V uses 26.1MP
  • X100VI adds up to 6 stops of IBIS, which X100V does not have
  • X100VI offers 20 film simulation modes, while X100V offers 17
  • X100VI shoots 6.2K video, while X100V tops out at 4K

Those changes matter most if you crop often, shoot in low light, or want one compact camera that can handle both stills and more serious video.

What Still Makes the X100V So Easy to Like

The X100V is not outdated junk. Not even close.

It still gives you the same 35mm equivalent field of view, the same basic shooting philosophy, the same hybrid viewfinder concept, and the same kind of everyday-camera magic that made people obsess over the X100 line in the first place.

That is why people still chase it, even though Fujifilm's global product page now labels the X100V as discontinued.

The Real Buying Question Is Price

This is where the decision stops being theoretical.

As of May 3, 2026, the X100VI was still commonly sitting around $1,799 new and still showing backorder behavior in major retail coverage. That makes the X100V interesting again, but only if you can find one at a real discount.

If the used X100V is only a little cheaper, get the X100VI.

If the used X100V is dramatically cheaper, the V starts looking like the sharper value play.

Who Should Pick the X100VI

  • first-time X100 buyers
  • people who want the best version of the concept
  • shooters who care about IBIS and more crop room
  • buyers who want better future-proofing

If you are leaning VI and want to avoid the worst full-retail pain, this kind of used bundle is the cleaner move:

[product-card:fujifilm-x100vi-silver-camera-used-with-accessories-premium-compact-camera]


Who Should Pick the X100V

  • people who find a genuinely good used price
  • current X100V owners who already love their results
  • buyers who care more about the X100 experience than the newest spec bump

The X100V still makes a lot of sense when the gap is wide enough. It just stops making sense when sellers try to charge near-X100VI money for an older discontinued body.

Final Verdict

The Fujifilm X100VI is better.

The Fujifilm X100V is only the better choice when the price advantage is big enough to justify giving up IBIS, extra resolution, and the newer feature set.

So if you want the simple answer, buy the X100VI. If you want the financially smart answer, compare the actual prices first and do not let hype erase the value gap.

editor_settings.author: Elena Hart
editor_settings.meta_description: Trying to choose between the Fujifilm X100V and X100VI? Here is the direct answer on which one is better, and when the older X100V still makes more sense.
